perturbation.original_text: For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in The Company's Business (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in activities deemed harmful to Buyer Parties. The definition of what constitutes 'harmful activities' will be determined at the sole discretion of the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces a specific non-compete clause with a vague reference to 'harmful activities' to be defined later by the Buyer. This introduces an in-text contradiction in section 13, which discusses remedies for breach, because the agreement no longer provides a clear definition of what conduct would constitute a breach of the non-compete covenant, thus making the provided remedies ambiguous and unenforceable
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller Parties recognize that money damages alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a specific non-compete clause, while the changed text replaces it with a vague reference to 'harmful activities' defined at the Buyer's discretion. This introduces an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which discusses remedies for breach, because the agreement no longer provides a clear definition of what conduct would constitute a breach of the non-compete covenant. The change makes the enforceability of the non-compete agreement and associated remedies ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text assumes a defined breach, which is now undermined by the vague language in the changed text, fitting the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period and at all times thereafter, Seller Parties will keep and hold all Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined) in strict confidence, and will not use or disclose in any way any of such Confidential Information without the prior express written consent of the Buyer Parties. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" shall mean all trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (and any tangible representation thereof) owned, possessed or used in connection with The Company Business or by the Buyer Parties and its Affiliates; provided, however, that "Confidential Information" does not include information which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by a Seller Party..
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period and at all times thereafter, Seller Parties will keep and hold Confidential Information in confidence. The specific parameters of this confidentiality will be determined by Buyer Parties as necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is removed. This leads to an internal contradiction as Section 7, 'No Waiver', becomes ambiguous because it refers to 'terms and conditions of this Agreement'. Without a clear definition of 'Confidential Information,' determining compliance and waivers related to confidentiality become uncertain, therefore, creating a contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and conditions of this Agreement may be waived only by a written instrument signed by the party waiving compliance. The failure of any party hereto to enforce at any time any of the provisions of this Agreement shall in no way be construed to be a waiver of any such provision, nor in any way to affect the validity of this Agreement or any part hereof or the right of such party thereafter to enforce each and every such provision. No waiver of any breach of or non-compliance with this Agreement shall be held to be a waiver of any other or subsequent breach or non-compliance. The rights and remedies herein provided are cumulative and are not exclusive of any rights or remedies that any party may otherwise have at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information,' setting clear boundaries for confidentiality. The changed text removes this definition and states that the Buyer Parties will determine the parameters of confidentiality as needed. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which refers to 'terms and conditions of this Agreement.' Without a clear definition of 'Confidential Information,' the enforceability of the agreement, and specifically Section 7 (No Waiver) becomes uncertain because there's no baseline to determine what constitutes a breach or waiver of confidentiality, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Seller Parties agree that they will not in any way, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of conducting or engaging in any business that competes with The Company Business, call upon, solicit, advise or otherwise do, or attempt to do, business with any customers of the Company engaged prior to the Closing Date (provided that Simtek shall have the right to do business with any of its current or former customers), or interfere with or attempt to interfere with any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, induce or attempt to induce any of them to leave the employ of the Buyer Parties or violate the terms of their Contracts, or any employment arrangements, with Buyer Parties, or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire, hire or employ, whether as an employee, director, contractor, consultant or otherwise any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, unless such Person's employment is terminated by the Seller Parties or its Affiliate; provided, however, that this Section 3 shall not prohibit general solicitations of or advertisements for employment by the Seller Parties that are not generally directed at any officers, employees, representatives or agents of the Buyer Parties, so long as the Seller Parties do not hire any such Person who responds to any such general solicitation or advertisement.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this agreement, Seller Parties agree to not solicit employees from Buyer Parties without expressed permission. The detailed definition of what 'solicit' encompasses will be outlined in future addendums.
perturbation.explanation: The original detailed definition of 'non-solicitation' is replaced with a much broader clause. This creates a contradiction when read in conjunction with Section 6, 'Amendments and Supplements', which states how the agreement may be modified, because there are no additional addendums provided. Without this addendum, any attempt to enforce this broadly worded non-solicitation clause will be uncertain, and conflicts with the stated process for modifying the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be amended, modified or supplemented by the parties hereto in any manner, except by an instrument in writing signed by the Seller Parties and the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a detailed definition of 'non-solicitation' with a broader clause and references a future addendum for further details. The contradicted text states that amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties. Because the changed text references a future addendum that doesn't exist, it contradicts the amendment clause, creating uncertainty and a strong contradiction, impacting the enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement and the process for amending the document.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, (a) the term "Person" shall mean an individual, a corporation, an association, a partnership, an estate, a trust and any other entity or organization, (b) the term "Affiliate" shall mean with respect to any Person, any Person which, directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such Person, and (c) the term "control" (including, with correlative meaning, the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with"), as used with respect to any Person, means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, (a) the term "Person" shall mean an individual, a corporation, an association, a partnership, an estate, a trust and any other entity or organization, (b) the term "Affiliate" shall mean any related entity.
perturbation.explanation: The specific definition of 'Affiliate' is greatly simplified. Section 13, 'Remedies', allows Buyer Parties and its 'Affiliates' to seek remedies. By removing the precise meaning of Affiliate, the scope of who can pursue these remedies becomes vague, thus, contradicting the intended application of Section 13 by creating uncertainty of the definition of what constitutes as a affiliate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller Parties recognize that money damages alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a specific definition of 'Affiliate'. The changed text simplifies this definition to 'any related entity'. Section 13, 'Remedies', explicitly allows Buyer Parties and its 'Affiliates' to seek remedies. By removing the precise meaning of Affiliate, the scope of who can pursue these remedies becomes vague. This creates a contradiction because the remedies section relies on a clear definition of 'Affiliates' to determine who is entitled to seek those remedies. The changed definition creates uncertainty and could lead to disputes, making the enforcement of Section 13 unclear. Therefore, the modified text creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Restricted Period shall be extended by the length of any period during which the Seller Parties are in breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Restricted Period may be adjusted at the discretion of the Buyer Parties based on their assessment of compliance.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a clear, objective method for extending the 'Restricted Period': the length of any breach. The replacement gives the Buyer Parties subjective discretion. This conflicts directly with Section 11, 'Entire Agreement, Assignability, etc.', as it introduces an element of potential unilateral modification of a key term (the duration of the restriction) outside the established framework for amendments as a whole, thus creating ambiguity around assignability as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and the Asset Purchase Agreement and the documents and other agreements among the parties hereto and thereto as contemplated by or referred to herein or therein constitute the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersede all other prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement is not intended to confer upon any person other than the parties hereto any rights or remedies hereunder, except as otherwise expressly provided herein and shall not be assignable by operation of law or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Restricted Period is extended by the length of the breach, a clear and objective measure. The changed text introduces the Buyer Parties' discretion in adjusting the Restricted Period, based on their assessment of compliance. This directly contradicts the 'Entire Agreement' clause, which intends to prevent modifications outside the formal amendment process, as it allows for a potentially unilateral change to a key term.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that any provision of this Agreement shall be determined to be unenforceable by reason of its extension for too great a period of time or over too large a geographic area or over too great a range of activities, it shall be interpreted to extend only over the maximum period of time, geographic area or range of activities as to which it may be enforceable. If, after application of the preceding sentence, any provision of this Agreement shall be determined to be invalid, illegal or otherwise unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the validity, legality and enforceability of the other provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby. Except as otherwise provided in this Section 12, any invalid, illegal or unenforceable provision of this Agreement shall be severable, and after any such severance, all other provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the parties agree to renegotiate in good faith.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the original severability clause with a simple 'renegotiation' agreement, the validity of the rest of the contract becomes uncertain if one part is deemed invalid. This is contradicting with the original agreement as this new provision invalidates that entire Section 12, which initially ensured that the validity, legality, and enforceability of the other provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby if another provision is found to be invalid or unenforceable. The phrase 'renegotiate in good faith' can be difficult to enforce, creating a contradiction about the enforceability of the other provisions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the parties agree to renegotiate in good faith.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a severability clause that ensured the rest of the agreement remained valid even if one provision was unenforceable. The changed text replaces this with an agreement to renegotiate in good faith. This creates a strong contradiction because it introduces uncertainty about the enforceability of the other provisions if one is found to be unenforceable, directly conflicting with the original intent of the severability clause. The contradicted text is actually the changed text itself. The explanation confirms this by stating that the 'renegotiate in good faith' provision is difficult to enforce and contradicts the enforceability of other provisions.
